question: Is the monitor screen displaying the grid of brain CT scans from Image 2?
Are the green measurement lines, crosshair targets, and text panels from Image 3 visible on the monitor screen?
Are the UI elements from Image 3 correctly superimposed over the CT scan images as a top layer?
Is the new screen content perspective-warped to match the specific angle and tilt of the monitor in Image 1?
Is the inserted content strictly contained within the monitor's bezel, ensuring no pixels bleed onto the black frame or background?
Are the original strip-shaped light reflections on the monitor glass from Image 1 preserved and visible on top of the new CT scan content?
Does the screen content exhibit realistic physical characteristics (e.g., slight pixel texture, moiré pattern) rather than appearing as a perfectly sharp digital overlay?
Do the black backgrounds of the CT scans appear slightly luminescent or dark gray, consistent with a photographed LCD screen, rather than pure digital black?
Is the large CT scanner machine on the left side of the room completely unchanged?
Are the keyboard, mouse, and papers/sticky notes on the desk preserved without distortion or unintended edits?
Is the monitor bezel and stand structure preserved intact, without warping or structural changes?
Are the text elements and measurement values on the screen legible and free from severe distortion?
Is the edited screen area free of visual artifacts such as visible seams, ghosting, or color banding?
Does the overall image convincingly appear as a single photograph of a medical workstation, with natural integration of the screen content?
